trades.catalyst: Market fully pricing out cuts due to Iran war oil shock and sticky inflation
trades.risk_notes: Oil price retreat or geopolitical de-escalation could revive cut expectations

trades.market: CPI above 3.3% for year ending March 2026
trades.catalyst: February CPI at 2.4% YoY gives little base effect room for 3.3%+ March outcome
trades.risk_notes: Oil shock effects could manifest sooner than expected in March reading

trades.market: CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026
trades.catalyst: Historical March CPI prints rarely exceed 0.9% MoM; oil effects likely delayed
trades.risk_notes: Unprecedented oil shock could drive extreme monthly print

trades.market: Miami high temperature above 81°F on April 7, 2026
trades.catalyst: Miami April historical averages show 80-82°F typical highs; no unusual weather patterns
trades.risk_notes: Unusual weather front could suppress temperatures

avoid.reason: Spread too wide >0.01 or conflicting with zero-cut thesis
Insufficient edge given current uncertainty and oil shock timing
Insufficient current information to gauge Q1 2026 performance with confidence

notes: Current macro regime is inflationary due to Iran war driving oil above $110/barrel and market repricing of Fed policy. Geopolitical tensions creating supply shocks that complicate disinflation narrative. Core inflation showing persistence while energy effects building. Markets have dramatically repriced Fed expectations with CME FedWatch showing 99.5% hold probability for April meeting.
